description: Fix TheTopbar: remove status dot, fix search box to open command palette, fix keybind display to show platform-aware shortcut (Ctrl+K / Cmd+K). Make search box click trigger command palette open event instead of being an independent search input.
steps: In panel/panel/web/admin/src/components/TheTopbar.vue: Remove the entire .status-dot div element and its associated CSS (.status-dot, .status-dot.offline, @keyframes livePulse).
Remove the `realtimeConnected` prop from the Props interface since it is no longer needed in the topbar.

Replace the independent search input logic with a simple clickable search-box that emits 'open-command-palette' when clicked. Remove searchQuery, searchExpanded, searchHovered, searchFocused, showSearchBar, onSearchFocus, onSearchBlur, handleSearchKeyup - replace with a single click handler that emits 'open-command-palette'.

Change the <kbd> shortcut display from hardcoded '⌘K' to a computed value that detects platform: show 'Cmd+K' on macOS, 'Ctrl+K' on other platforms. Use navigator.platform or navigator.userAgent to detect Mac.

Simplify the search-box markup: make it a static display that shows the search icon, 'Search...' text, and the platform-aware shortcut kbd - always visible, not expanding/collapsing. Clicking anywhere on it emits 'open-command-palette'.

In panel/panel/web/admin/src/layouts/AppShell.vue: Remove the :realtime-connected prop from TheTopbar usage. Add @open-command-palette handler that opens the command palette (import and use the useCommandPalette composable or expose an open method).

In panel/panel/web/admin/src/layouts/AppShell.vue: To connect search box click to CommandPalette, add a ref or provide/inject pattern. The simplest approach: add a global event bus or use a shared ref. Since CommandPalette.vue already uses useCommandPalette which exposes open(), expose it via a template ref or use a shared composable instance. Best approach: make useCommandPalette a singleton by moving its state to module scope, then AppShell can import and call open().
Make the search-box responsive for mobile: on small screens, show only the search icon (hide text and kbd shortcut). Add @media (max-width: 640px) responsive styles.
acceptance_criteria: No red/green status dot appears in the topbar
Clicking the search box opens the command palette overlay
The shortcut hint shows 'Ctrl+K' on non-Mac platforms and 'Cmd+K' on Mac
The search box is always visible (not expanding/collapsing on hover)
On mobile viewports (<640px), the search box shows only the icon
